board.title: Hypotheses
board.description: Testable R&D hypotheses for your goals: mechanism, evidence, risk, value and an experiment plan.
board.loadPageError: Failed to load the next page
board.emptyFiltered: Nothing matches the current filters. Change the search terms or reset the filters.
board.emptyQueue: This queue is empty for now — pick another queue above.

tabHints.hypotheses: A hypothesis is a testable assumption about what to improve and how, built on the documents in your knowledge base. The system verifies each one against the base and estimates technology readiness — cards update automatically.
tabHints.directions: A direction is a promising research area found automatically in the topics of your base. It is a hint for choosing a goal: concrete hypotheses can be generated from a direction.
tabHints.drafts: A draft is a hypothesis stub that does not yet have enough supporting evidence in the base. It can be refined into a full hypothesis or deleted.
tabHints.graph: The knowledge graph shows which documents each hypothesis relies on: green links support it, red links contradict it. Shared documents connect related hypotheses.
tabHints.methodology: How the system creates, verifies and scores hypotheses — step by step.

hypotheses.title: No hypotheses yet
hypotheses.text: Create a goal and run generation — hypotheses will appear here with evidence and a rating.

directions.title: No directions yet
directions.text: Directions are assembled automatically from the topics of the knowledge base. Upload more documents — and the system will find promising areas.

drafts.title: No drafts
drafts.text: Rejected and unfinished hypotheses end up here. There are none for now — everything is in progress on the Hypotheses tab.

needsVerify.label: Under review
needsVerify.hint: The system is collecting arguments for and against

risk.label: Contradictions
risk.hint: There are arguments against — needs a closer look

insufficient.label: Not enough evidence
insufficient.hint: Verification finished: the corpus holds too little evidence for or against. It won't change on its own — add sources and re-check, refine the wording, or close it manually.

generate.title: Generate hypotheses
generate.description: The system will study the knowledge-base documents and propose testable ways to reach the selected goal.
generate.goalOnly: Hypotheses will be created for this goal.
generate.goalTitlePlaceholder: e.g. reduce copper losses to flotation tailings
generate.contextPlaceholder: site, ore type and production specifics
generate.constraintsPlaceholder: what is allowed and what is not: budget, equipment, reagents, sales…
generate.constraintsHint: The system will not propose hypotheses that violate these constraints.
generate.backgroundNote: Generation runs in the background and takes one–two minutes — finished hypotheses will appear on the board by themselves; you can close this window.
generate.dataNote: Hypotheses are built from the documents in your knowledge base. Add your reports, flowsheets and regulations on the Documents page.

digest.dialogDescription: A summary of the hypotheses matching the selected filters — copy it or download as a file.
digest.heading: Hypothesis digest ({{n}})

belief.label: Confidence in the hypothesis
belief.hint: How strongly the scientific corpus supports that the hypothesis is true.

verdict.label: Confidence in the verification
verdict.hint: How reliable the verification conclusion itself is.

evidence.label: Evidence quality
evidence.hint: How strong the sources behind the conclusion are.

methodology.howDescription: The path from documents to a testable hypothesis.

parsing.title: Parsing the knowledge base
parsing.text: Documents are split into fragments, cleaned of technical noise and linked to their source — every conclusion can be opened at the exact place in the article or report.

topics.title: Topics and directions
topics.text: The system groups similar fragments into topics and sketches directions on its own — a map of promising areas. This is reconnaissance over the corpus, not proven hypotheses.

generation.title: Generating hypotheses for a goal
generation.text: For a goal, relevant fragments are retrieved, materials, processes and properties are extracted, and a testable link is formed: what to change, where to apply it and which indicator measures the effect.

ranking.title: Verification and ranking
ranking.text: The rating is computed with a transparent formula from goal fit, evidence strength, novelty, value, risk and technology readiness. An expert can change the status and refine the passport.

stances.text: "For" — the fragment supports the hypothesis, "against" — refutes it, "mentions" — on topic but without a direct conclusion. Until the hypothesis is verified against the base, the counters stay empty.

verdict.text: "Supported" — there is support with no serious contradictions; "refuted" — refutations prevail; "contradictory" — both were found; "insufficient data" — too little on the topic.

evidenceGraph.text: Connects hypotheses to specific documents and fragments. Every edge is marked as for, against or mention — you can open the source and check the exact place.

knowledgeGraph.text: Connects not articles by citations but extracted chains material → process → property → goal. New directions are born from their intersections.

ranking.group: What the rating is made of

kpiFit.text: How much the hypothesis actually moves the chosen indicator.

evidenceBase.text: How many independent fragments were found and whether there are contradictions.

novelty.text: How much the solution differs from already known works and patents.

valueRisk.text: The potential payoff against technical limitations and side effects.

readiness.text: How close the idea is to a laboratory test on the TRL scale.

methodology.aiNote: The system speeds up the start of R&D: it collects arguments and options, but does not replace engineering expertise, a test plan and checking the original sources.
methodology.weightsDescription: Choose what matters most when prioritizing your hypotheses — the weights apply to your portfolio on the next board load.

graph.loadError: Failed to load the knowledge graph
graph.emptyTitle: The graph is empty for now
graph.emptyText: Generate hypotheses — they will appear here together with the documents they rely on.
graph.quickNavHint: Tap a hypothesis — its sources light up in the graph and its evidence opens on the right
graph.noRelated: No related nodes in the current view.
graph.aria: Knowledge graph: hypotheses and source documents

report.title: Hypothesis portfolio report
report.summary: Hypotheses in the portfolio: {{total}} · approved by experts: {{approved}} · goals: {{goals}}
report.rankingNote: Priority follows a transparent formula: goal fit, evidence, novelty, value, risk, readiness — taking the expert's decision into account.
report.truncated: Showing the top {{shown}} of {{total}}; the full list is in the CSV/JSON export.
report.footer: This report was generated automatically by the Hypothesis Factory. Novelty, value and risk scores are model-based and require expert validation; every hypothesis links to its sources in the system.
report.execNote: Key technology themes of the corpus by the technology index (TechScore).
report.pilotsNote: Portfolio top by priority — candidates for the first experiments.

hreport.disclaimer: Novelty, value, risk and readiness scores are model-estimated and require expert validation; every claim is backed by source references.

a.result: Automated corpus check result: {{verdict}} ({{forCount}} for, {{againstCount}} against).
a.resultPending: The automated corpus check had not finished when this report was generated.

hreport.abbreviations: Abbreviations: TRL — technology readiness level; ITC — technology index; KPI — key performance indicator.
hreport.causalTableTitle: Causal chain composition → process → microstructure → property → KPI
hreport.unverifiedNote: Automated verification is not finished — treat the scores with caution.
hreport.noEvidence: No corpus evidence collected yet; the automated check had not finished when this report was generated.
hreport.checkPending: The automated check is still running — the corpus fragments collected so far are listed below.
hreport.noAgainst: No contradicting fragments found in the corpus.
hreport.moreQuotes: {{count}} more supporting fragments are available in the system.
hreport.conclVerdictPending: The automated corpus check is not finished — the verdict will be refined.
hreport.recReview: investigate further before an expert decision
hreport.systemLink: Full source fragments, the hypothesis graph and history are available in the system: {{path}}
